audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Peter text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for shepherding elders, no domination, humility, anxiety cast on Divine, adversarial vigilance, restoration, final greetings.

verses.restored: Therefore I appeal to elders among you, fellow elder and witness of Messiah's sufferings and partner of glory about to be unveiled:
shepherd the flock of the Divine among you, overseeing not by compulsion but willingly according to Divine, not for shameful gain but eagerly,
not as lording over those allotted to you, but becoming patterns for the flock.
And when the chief Shepherd is manifested, you will receive the unfading crown of glory.
Likewise younger ones, be arranged under elders; and all of you clothe yourselves with humility toward one another, because the Divine opposes arrogant ones but gives grace to humble ones.
Therefore be humbled under the mighty hand of the Divine, so that he may lift you in season,
casting all your anxiety upon him, because he cares concerning you.
Be sober; keep awake. Your adversary, the accuser, walks around like a roaring lion, seeking someone to swallow.
Resist him, firm in trust, knowing the same sufferings are being completed in your siblinghood in the world.
And the Divine of all grace, the One calling you into his age-long glory in Messiah, after you have suffered a little, will himself restore, establish, strengthen, and ground you.
To him be dominion into the ages. Amen.
Through Silvanus, the trustworthy sibling as I consider, I wrote briefly, appealing and testifying this to be true grace of the Divine. Stand in it.
She who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, greets you, and Mark my son.
Greet one another with kiss of love. Peace to all of you in Messiah.

translation_notes: Eldership/shepherding is explicitly non-domineering; leaders are patterns, not lords over the flock.
Anxiety is cast on Divine care, not dismissed.
The adversarial lion image is vigilance language; do not turn people into the enemy.
